steps.title: Spot the contrast between limitation and achievement
steps.content:
The first sentence establishes that the painting “doesn't technically represent a fourth dimension” (a limitation).

The second sentence then reveals what the painting actually does achieve through “multiple, simultaneous perspectives.” The transition must acknowledge the limitation while pivoting to this contrasting accomplishment.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “That said” is the concession-contrast transition—it acknowledges one point while moving to a different angle on it. The other three extend the idea, restate it, or exemplify it—none of which bridge ideas this way. B is correct.
